content: We can reframe intrinsic curiosity bonus calculation in ICM/RND to use a Transformer attention gating mechanism that only retrieves episodic memory traces of previously experienced surprising states to update prediction error, rather than comparing against all stored memory. This creates a self-calibrating curious agent that avoids the common asymptotic stagnation problem where familiar environments stop producing meaningful exploration bonuses.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
Transformer attention mechanism for episodic memory
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
